concerns.text: Task T033 generates 'statistical_framing.md' but lacks explicit traceability to SC-002 (Statistical inference framing). While the description mentions 'per SC-002', the task does not explicitly define the logic to check randomization status in the dataset metadata to determine if findings are 'associational' vs 'causal', risking incomplete coverage of the success criterion's measurement requirement.

Task T035 generates 'power_analysis.md' but lacks explicit traceability to SC-004 (Sample size and power). The task description mentions 'documenting sample size (n≥20)', but does not explicitly include the requirement to 'document the method used for power consideration' or 'acknowledge power limitations' as mandated by SC-004, leaving the success criterion partially unmet.

Task T034 generates 'sensitivity_report.md' but lacks explicit traceability to SC-005 (Threshold justification). While it mentions 'per SC-005', the task does not explicitly require the 'one-line rationale' for cutoffs or the 'sensitivity analysis sweeping the cutoff over a range of thresholds' for the p-value/FDR parameter, which is a core part of SC-005's definition of threshold justification.

Task T006 implements FD calculation and exclusion but lacks explicit coverage for the 'log the exclusion count' requirement found in spec.md Edge Cases and SC-004 (n≥20 threshold). The task description focuses on the calculation logic but omits the mandatory logging and potential halt-if-insufficient-sample logic required to satisfy the success criteria and edge case handling.

Task T012 describes preprocessing but allows 'nilearn CPU methods' as a fallback. This conflicts with FR-002 and Constitution Principle VI which mandate 'FSL or AFNI' pipelines for 'Neuroimaging Preprocessing Integrity'. The task does not explicitly enforce the version-locked FSL/AFNI requirement, creating a gap in coverage for the strict preprocessing integrity constraint.

Task T013 schedules 'delete raw NIfTI files immediately after successful preprocessing'. This directly contradicts Constitution Principle III ('Raw data is preserved unchanged') and Principle I ('Every result... reproducible by re-running... against the project's data/'). The task covers the disk space goal but fails to cover the mandatory data hygiene requirement to preserve raw data for reproducibility.
